worstScenarios.title: Lefty-Righty vs Switch Hitter
worstScenarios.concept: Against switch hitters, use your most effective reliever — handedness matters less than performance

worstScenarios.title: Two Runners Tagging
worstScenarios.concept: When two runners can tag, both should go — the defense can only make one throw
issues: Explanation 2 has garbled sentence structure ('Only you tagging is too conservative, which means...' doesn't flow logically)

strengths: Teaching simultaneous tagging strategy is valuable advanced concept
Options present realistic decisions runners might consider
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Fix score (home leads 6-5, not trails), rewrite explanation 3 to clarify R2 stays at 2nd if R3 doesn't tag, fix explanation 2's grammar, and clarify the scenario setup to make it clear both runners have separate bases to advance to

worstScenarios.title: Which Pitch to Expect
worstScenarios.concept: Look fastball first — it's the most common pitch. React to everything else

worstScenarios.title: Gun Down at Third
worstScenarios.concept: Right fielders with strong arms can keep runners from going first-to-third — that throw takes away a scoring-position runner.
issues: Explanation 1 has grammatical error ('because throwing to third base') and awkward structure

Concept teaches incorrect principle - preventing first-to-third is about positioning/charging, not arm strength
strengths: Clear game situation with specific details
Options are distinct and represent different strategic choices
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change scenario to runner on first with two outs on a ball hit to RF corner. Best option should be 'throw to second base to hold runner at first' (rate ~70). Teach that preventing extra bases is often smarter than attempting low-percentage throws.

worstScenarios.title: Third Base Leads — Coaching the Runner

worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Rewrite scenario with 3B covering the base for the tag play. The third BASE COACH (not baseman) makes the hold/go decision. Change concept to 'tag-plays' or 'defensive-positioning'.

worstScenarios.title: Appeal Play — Missing a Base

worstScenarios.title: Cutoff Decision

worstScenarios.title: Ground Rule Double

(1) Keep as is - it's good. (2) 'Ground rule doubles limit runners to two bases to be fair to the defense who couldn't field the ball.' (3) 'Baseball doesn't have do-overs - once the ball bounces over, the play stands with its automatic ruling.' Also change conceptTag to 'ground-rules'.

worstScenarios.title: Runner Threatening to Steal

Also differentiate options 1 and 4 more clearly, and explain why a bunt is happening on 0-0 count.

worstScenarios.title: Running Out a Double

worstScenarios.title: Ball in the Gap — First to Third?
worstScenarios.concept: On extra-base hits, round the base hard and watch your third base coach — he sees the whole play

worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Rewrite explanations to flow naturally: 'Sprinting blindly to third gets runners thrown out when outfielders make quick plays.' 'Rounding second hard while watching your coach gives you the best information - he sees whether the ball is cleanly fielded and where the throw is going.' 'Trying for home from first requires the ball to get completely past both outfielders - extremely rare even in the gap.' Adjust rates to 55/35/70/15 for more realistic probabilities.

worstScenarios.title: Day Game After Night Game

worstScenarios.title: Third Time Through the Order

Studies show batting average jumps .040+ points as hitters time your release and recognize spin. You need to disrupt their timing, not confirm it.'

strengths: Excellent teaching of count leverage with specific 3-0 strategy
Realistic scenario with proper context (wild pitcher, down by 2)
Clear distinction between options with perfect rate distribution

results.title: 2-0 Sit on Off-Speed
results.concept: General rules like 'sit fastball at 2-0' are starting points. Great hitters adjust to what the specific pitcher is showing them. At 2-0, you can afford to gamble because even a wrong guess leaves you ahead.
issues: Explanation 4 contains awkward phrasing: 'wasting the count advantage, which means' - should flow more naturally
Option 1 could be more compelling as a 'tempting wrong answer' - many coaches would still recommend fastball here
strengths: Excellent teaching of situational adjustment over rigid rules - perfect for ages 10+
Strong use of specific evidence (40% changeup usage, two straight changeups) to justify strategic thinking

results.title: Count-Based Stealing
results.concept: The best counts for stealing are when the pitcher is slightly behind (1-0, 2-1) because he's focused on throwing a strike to the batter and more likely to throw a predictable fastball.
issues: Option 2 explanation has awkward phrasing: 'delays too long, because' should be 'delays too long. The longer...' for better flow
strengths: Excellent teaching of count leverage for base stealing with clear, age-appropriate explanations
Perfect category alignment - this is exactly what a 'counts' scenario should teach
Strong positive teaching in best answer explaining WHY 1-0 is optimal timing

results.title: 1-2 Put-Away Pitch (Pitcher's View)
results.concept: Elite pitch sequencing at 1-2 means changing the look, not repeating what worked. Same location, different speed catches hitters who are adjusting to what they just saw.
issues: Explanation 4 needs better punctuation - it's a run-on sentence that could confuse younger readers
strengths: Excellent teach-through-scenario approach that shows real pitcher decision-making
Rates perfectly reflect realistic success probabilities for each pitch choice
Explanations teach distinct principles: predictability, playing to strengths, sequencing theory, and strike zone strategy

results.title: Full Count, Nobody On — Batter's Edge
results.concept: At 3-2, use the information from the entire at-bat. If a pitcher has been fastball-dominant and his secondary stuff is shaky, commit to the heater. Great hitters use evidence, not guesses.
issues: Third explanation uses 'because' mid-sentence creating awkward flow
Fourth explanation uses 'since' creating formulaic structure
strengths: Excellent use of at-bat history to teach evidence-based hitting decisions
Strong age-appropriate analytics approach for diff:3 players
